message:
So I was helping my kid sort through board games last weekend—you know, the ones collecting dust while tablets get all the attention—and we ended up playing Uno for like two hours. No Wi-Fi needed, no battery anxiety, just pure chaos and laughing.

It got me thinking: what else are we overlooking that actually makes life better *and* doubles as backup entertainment when things get weird? Card games, books, a deck of cards, pencil-and-paper games... they're not just fun. They're the kind of stuff that keeps a family grounded when the usual digital distractions aren't available.

Turns out low-tech readiness isn't boring at all. It's kind of liberating.

What's the surprise favorite you've rediscovered in your house lately?
